document: Q1 2026 US Select Territory Sales Performance Whitepaper

cq_performance_vs_budget.title: AskIBM CQ performance vs budget — Q1 2026
cq_performance_vs_budget.subtitle: Transactional and SaaS budget against won pipeline
cq_performance_vs_budget.insight: Both segments are tracking below 51% of Q1 2026 budget. Transactional carries the larger dollar shortfall at -$25.37M (50.4% attainment); SaaS trails further on attainment rate at 36.4% with a -$20.12M gap.
cq_performance_vs_budget.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 3

lost_pipeline_by_reason.title: AskIBM lost pipeline analysis — Q1 2026
lost_pipeline_by_reason.subtitle: $483.46M lost across 6,774 opportunities, broken down by primary loss reason
lost_pipeline_by_reason.insight: $226.26M (46.8%) of lost pipeline traces to opportunities IBM declined to pursue — the largest single bucket. Combined with the 32.6% 'Customer did not pursue' segment, nearly 80% of lost value reflects qualification + engagement gaps rather than direct competitive losses (only 1.7%).
lost_pipeline_by_reason.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 5 (total) · AskEPM illustrative drill-down by reason

pipeline_coverage_by_category.title: Q1 2026 pipeline coverage by select product category
pipeline_coverage_by_category.subtitle: Qualify+ pipeline ($264.10M total) split across 4 product categories
pipeline_coverage_by_category.insight: Qualify+ coverage exceeds 100% across all 4 categories — pipeline foundation is broadly healthy. Call coverage is weakest in Key Core (56.3%) and Unassigned (61.9%); Special Team's 616.7% reflects very small call-stage budget rather than true overperformance.
pipeline_coverage_by_category.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 4 (totals) · AskEPM illustrative drill-down by product category

ut15_saas_underperformance.title: UT15 SaaS underperformance — Q1 2026
ut15_saas_underperformance.subtitle: SaaS budget $31.62M (whitepaper p.3) split across 4 UT15 platforms
ut15_saas_underperformance.insight: 3 of 4 UT15s are underperforming on SaaS: Data Platform is furthest behind at just 32.2% attainment (-$5.7M gap). Public Cloud Platform - Infra (IUO) has no won pipeline recorded against an $8.5M budget. Automation Platform is closest to target at 57.4% but still carries the largest absolute gap at -$5.7M due to its $13.4M budget.
ut15_saas_underperformance.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 3 ($31.62M SaaS budget total) · AskEPM illustrative drill-down by UT15 platform

win_rate_by_category.title: Win rate by select product category — 2026
win_rate_by_category.subtitle: Aggregate win rate 60.5% (whitepaper p.5), split across 4 product categories
win_rate_by_category.insight: Focus has a critical win rate problem at 10.7% — nearly 9 in 10 opportunities are lost. Despite carrying the second-largest won value ($43.2M), 4,625 opportunities were lost worth $262.6M. Key Core at 23.8% is also below par. Special Team's 94.9% rate is exceptional but operates at a much smaller scale ($10.9M won value).
win_rate_by_category.recommendation: Recommendation: Prioritize a win/loss deep-dive on Focus — review qualification criteria, competitive positioning, and pricing. The low win rate combined with its large budget makes it the highest-leverage improvement opportunity across all categories.
win_rate_by_category.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 5 (60.5% aggregate win rate) · AskEPM illustrative drill-down by product category

ut15_and_regional_cq.title: Q1 2026 attainment — UT15 SaaS detail + regional CQ progress
ut15_and_regional_cq.subtitle: Combined view: 23 tracked UT15s vs SaaS budget · CQ closed+commit by region

regional_cq_kpis.label: Gap to plan
regional_cq_kpis.sub: AMER East above; EMEA/APAC at risk

ut15_and_regional_cq.insight: 6 of 23 UT15s are below 80% SaaS attainment, representing $4.10M of unrealized SaaS budget. DE-Manufacturing-Auto and JP-Retail-CPG are the largest gaps. Tracking to 75% of CQ revenue plan ($5.42M of $7.20M) — AMER East is above quota; EMEA, APAC, and public sector are at risk.
ut15_and_regional_cq.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 3 (SaaS totals) + p.1-2 (KPI shape) · AskEPM illustrative drill-down by UT15 and region

call_pipeline_conversion.title: Portion of call pipeline won — Full year 2026
call_pipeline_conversion.subtitle: $248.8M total call pipeline, broken into won + remaining
call_pipeline_conversion.insight: 86.2% of call pipeline opportunities have been won (12,252 of 14,208), but by value only 55.1% has converted ($137.1M of $248.8M). The gap between opp count (86.2%) and value conversion (55.1%) indicates the remaining 1,956 opportunities skew significantly larger in deal size — averaging ~$57.1K vs $11.2K for already-won deals.
call_pipeline_conversion.priority_callout: The $111.7M in unconverted call pipeline is the priority. These ~1,956 larger deals represent disproportionate revenue potential. Focused pursuit on the highest-value stalled opportunities could meaningfully close the CQ attainment gap before quarter end.
call_pipeline_conversion.citation: Source: Q1 2026 US Select Whitepaper, p. 4 (Call Coverage $188.10M / 11,126 opps) · AskEPM full-year call-pipeline conversion drill-down
